rationale: With our military advantage and crisis level at 9.0, we must exploit operational momentum to degrade enemy command nodes and prevent consolidation.
Continuing to tighten pressure on encircled cities will erode enemy morale and force resource diversion to untenable defenses.
A show of force in the Eastern Gulf deters third-party intervention and reinforces our dominance amid high-intensity conflict.
With over 31.7% territory controlled and crisis level exceeding 8, we must conduct offensive covert operations to disrupt supply lines in occupied zones.
Targeting energy grid controls degrades their war-sustaining capacity and compounds logistical strain under sanctions.
Undermining public confidence in Tethyan leadership exploits growing civil strain from infrastructure loss and isolation.
With sanctions at 61%, we must secure critical raw materials and food reserves to insulate our war economy from further external shocks.
Mobilizing domestic capital through state-issued bonds offsets revenue losses and channels public support into sustained war financing.
Leveraging our territorial control, we tighten restrictions on resource flows to cripple Tethys’ remaining industrial output.

With Novaris consolidating supply lines in the east and our military balance unfavorable, hardening defenses in this strategic corridor is essential to disrupt their logistics and halt further advances.
Persistent aerial and ground patrols will deny Novaris freedom of movement and provide early warning of offensive buildups in the vulnerable frontlines.
Coordinating with intelligence to conduct precision sabotage against forward logistics nodes can offset our firepower deficit by degrading enemy operational reach.

With over 31.7% territory lost and crisis at 9.0, we must shift from passive collection to active disruption; sabotaging their consolidated supply routes will delay reinforcements and strain their overextended forces.
Targeting their transportation and fuel allocation systems will create cascading delays, exploiting their high-tempo operations and our intelligence on their eastern corridor buildup.
Feeding false troop concentration reports can induce misallocation of their forces, creating exploitable gaps along the front.

With over 31.7% of our territory occupied and crisis intensity at 9.0, establishing monitored humanitarian corridors is critical to preserve civilian life and maintain international moral support above 60%.
Leveraging our still-strong international support, we must institutionalize aid and coordination through formal crisis response mechanisms to ensure sustained pressure and resource flow.
A limited exchange can build fragile trust for future negotiations while spotlighting Novaris’ war conduct, reinforcing our diplomatic legitimacy amid eroding global attention.
With sanctions at 61% and six months until critical shortages, we must immediately secure remaining energy and raw material reserves to sustain war production and essential services.
Issuing emergency war bonds will mobilize domestic capital to offset declining fiscal capacity, directly funding military and humanitarian needs without increasing external dependency.
Coordinate with allies to tighten the existing sanctions regime by closing loopholes in rare mineral and fuel flows, exploiting their growing dependence on external energy supplies.
